key_risks: A softer-than-expected CPI/PPI and dovish FOMC could trigger a sharp rebound in mega-cap technology and Nasdaq exposure, causing this value/defensive tilt to lag.
Oil prices could fall on weaker demand data or easing geopolitical risk, hurting the energy allocation.
Healthcare could reverse if policy headlines, reimbursement concerns, or profit-taking follow its recent strong move.
Financials and regional credit sentiment could deteriorate if yield-curve, commercial real estate, or deposit concerns re-emerge.
Broad risk-off conditions around inflation or labor data could pull all equity sectors down despite defensive tilts.

portfolio.rationale: Recent relative strength and defensive characteristics should help if hot inflation data or the FOMC pressures high-duration growth equities.
High oil price backdrop and persistent inflation favor energy-sector earnings and provide a hedge against renewed commodity or geopolitical risk.
Value-oriented exposure with recent positive momentum; higher-for-longer rates and proposed capital requirement relief can support bank and capital-markets sentiment.
Defense budget and geopolitical-risk support offer idiosyncratic demand drivers with better recent trend than broad equities.
Adds diversified US equity exposure while reducing mega-cap growth concentration after recent Nasdaq and technology weakness.

portfolio_rationale: The allocation tilts away from rate-sensitive mega-cap growth and toward sectors with recent relative strength, inflation/geopolitical hedges, and value/defensive characteristics. It seeks to outperform the S&P 500 over a macro-heavy month with CPI, PPI, FOMC, retail sales, and labor data all inside the scoring window.
rationale_summary: Hot inflation readings and a likely higher-for-longer policy backdrop make energy, financials, healthcare, and defense more attractive than concentrated growth for the next month. Equal-weight exposure provides some participation if breadth improves without relying heavily on the largest S&P 500 technology names.
